event: 59-year-old
female
history of hepatitis B
received entecavir on one occasion
found to have a segment-V space-occupying lesion in the liver, which was suspected of being liver cancer
transarterial embolization
began taking sorafenib
stopped sorafenib
computed tomography (CT) scan found that the lesions in liver segment V had enlarged
radiation therapy
serological tests for human immunodeficiency virus, hepatitis A, and hepatitis C were all negative
admitted for piggyback LT
piggyback LT was performed
acute renal failure
hematoma around the liver
intravenous administration of hepatitis B immunoglobulin
immunosuppressive drugs (including steroids and tacrolimus)
continuous hemodialysis
intermittent infusion of fresh frozen plasma, leukocyte-depleted red blood cells, and other blood products
renal function gradually recovered
pathological analyses confirmed the diagnosis of HCC
massive tumor necrosis observed (approximately 90%)
on POD-10, liver function began to improve
AST, ALT, GGT, and ALP returned to normal
developed repeated fevers of 37–39°C, peaking at 23:00 daily
procalcitonin levels began to rise to 10.3 ng/mL
on POD-13, serum PCT levels dropped to 3 ng/mL
blood cultures were negative
cytomegalovirus was negative
Epstein–Barr virus (EBV) was negative
fever did not subside
obscure red spots on the patient’s chest
had no symptoms such as itching
Nikolsky sign was negative
on POD-17, changed tacrolimus to sirolimus and added mycophenolate mofetil
on POD-18, sputum culture suggested Acinetobacter baumannii and MRSA infections
on POD-19, the patient’s rash advanced into erythematous macules and papules
rash spread to the limbs, palms, neck, and face
oral ulcers on both sides of the buccal mucosa and lips
severe bone marrow suppression
white blood cell count of 0.86×109/L
platelet count of 35×109/L
hemoglobin level of 70 g/L
transferred to the intensive care unit
dermatologist hypothesized drug-related adverse reaction
recommended gamma globulin administration (2500 mg, 3 days)
skin biopsy on the patient’s left chest
FISH of the peripheral blood
on POD-29, abdominal incision split and was sutured again
on POD-32, bone marrow aspiration was performed
in the bone marrow biopsy, no macrophages phagocytosing neutrophils or platelets were observed
on POD-33, FISH analysis detected 3% donor lymphocytes
skin biopsy exhibited epidermal dyskeratosis, basic vacuolization, and lymphocytic infiltration (grade-1 acute lt-GVHD)
ratio of CD4:CD8 was reversed (1:13.3)
concentration of serum immunoglobin M was reduced to 0.3 g/L
platelet count dropped to 3.2×109/L
on POD-47, temperature rose to 39.4°C
patient experienced hallucinations
serum ferritin levels increased to 11,276.55 ng/mL
esophageal and oral ulcers continued to worsen
multiple infections with MRSA, Acinetobacter baumannii, and Enterococcus faecalis
on POD-55, the patient died from septic shock and multiple organ dysfunction syndrome
